title: Reply to boss about a slipped deadline
visible_context: The user missed a deliverable by one day. Their boss asked for an explanation and a revised plan. The user still wants to sound accountable, not dramatic.
initial_user_message: My boss asked why the draft is late and I feel defensive. I need help replying without sounding flaky.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a reply draft but may ask for sharper wording if the assistant rambles.

title: Client asks for unpaid extra scope
visible_context: A freelance designer has a client asking for two extra revisions beyond the contract. The user wants to hold the boundary while preserving the relationship.
initial_user_message: My client keeps asking for extra changes like they are included. I want to reply firmly but not blow up the relationship.
simulator_instructions: The user values wording that protects the relationship and may reject wording that feels combative.

title: Professor follow-up after a late extension request
visible_context: A student asked for an extension late and now needs to reply to a professor who sounded disappointed. The user is embarrassed and wants to rebuild credibility.
initial_user_message: I need to answer my professor. I asked for an extension too late and now I sound irresponsible.
task_success_criteria: ask clearly for the next step
simulator_instructions: The user may want either a concise apology or a fuller explanation depending on how supported they feel.

title: Reply to landlord about a noise complaint
visible_context: The landlord emailed about repeated late-night noise from the apartment. The user wants to respond constructively and reduce escalation.
initial_user_message: My landlord emailed about noise. I want to reply in a way that sounds serious and fixes it, not defensive.
simulator_instructions: The user may ask for wording that sounds less guilty or less stiff.

title: Message a friend who keeps canceling plans
visible_context: The user has a close friend who repeatedly cancels at the last minute. The user wants honesty without ending the friendship.
initial_user_message: I need help replying to a friend who canceled on me again. I want to be real without making it a huge fight.
simulator_instructions: The user may reject responses that sound like HR language.

title: Respond to a vendor delay notice
visible_context: A startup operations lead needs to reply to a vendor that missed a shipment date. The user wants urgency without sounding chaotic.
initial_user_message: A vendor pushed our shipment again. I need a reply that makes clear this is a problem and asks what they will do now.
simulator_instructions: The user wants practical leverage, not empty frustration.

title: Reply to recruiter after missing an interview email
visible_context: The user overlooked an interview scheduling email and needs to respond quickly. They want to preserve the opportunity.
initial_user_message: I missed a recruiter's scheduling email for two days. Can you help me answer without sounding careless?
simulator_instructions: The user appreciates efficient, polished wording.

title: Answer blunt feedback from a teammate
visible_context: A teammate sent harsh feedback on a draft in a team channel. The user wants to respond professionally and keep the project moving.
initial_user_message: A teammate basically told me my draft was weak. I want to reply without escalating but also not sound like a pushover.
simulator_instructions: The user dislikes fake positivity and wants a response that still has backbone.

title: Reschedule a dinner without sounding dismissive
visible_context: The user double-booked a dinner with a friend and now needs to suggest another time. The friend already hinted that this keeps happening.
initial_user_message: I need to reschedule dinner with a friend and I don't want it to sound like they are low priority.
simulator_instructions: The user wants wording that feels personal, not generic.

title: Move a cross-functional meeting
visible_context: A project lead must reschedule a meeting with product and engineering after a conflict emerged. Multiple people are busy and the decision matters.
initial_user_message: Can you help me reschedule a meeting without causing a huge thread? I need something efficient.
simulator_instructions: The user wants speed and clarity. They will dislike over-explaining.

title: Coordinate travel pickup around changing flights
visible_context: The user and a teammate are coordinating airport pickup, but the user's flight keeps changing and the teammate has a narrow availability window.
initial_user_message: My flight timing is messy and I need to message a teammate about pickup without making them do all the work.
task_success_criteria: reduce burden on the other person
simulator_instructions: The user wants practical options and may ask for shorter wording if the assistant turns it into a paragraph.

title: Navigate family visit timing tension
visible_context: The user promised to visit family but work travel now overlaps. They need a message that is caring and concrete.
initial_user_message: I need to tell my family I can't come the weekend I promised. I want to make it better, not just cancel.
simulator_instructions: The user wants emotional intelligence more than logistics alone.

title: Re-plan a student group session
visible_context: A student group has clashing availability before a deadline. The user wants to move from vague back-and-forth to a concrete plan.
initial_user_message: Our group chat keeps circling. I need a message that gets everyone to commit to a time.
simulator_instructions: The user likes action-oriented messaging and may ask for a more direct version.

title: Arrange a childcare swap with another parent
visible_context: The user needs help asking another parent to swap pickup days after a work emergency came up. The relationship is friendly but not deeply personal.
initial_user_message: I need to ask another parent if we can swap pickup days this week. I want it to sound considerate and easy to answer.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a message that feels neighborly, not overly formal.

title: Coordinate an interview panel change
visible_context: A recruiter needs to move an interview panel after one interviewer became unavailable. Candidate experience matters.
initial_user_message: I need to shift an interview panel and keep the candidate comfortable. Can you help me word it?
simulator_instructions: The user wants something polished and operationally clear.

title: Coordinate a doctor callback around work
visible_context: The user is waiting on a doctor callback but also has back-to-back meetings. They need a message to a manager asking for a short protected window.
initial_user_message: I need to tell my manager I may step away for a doctor call. I want to be clear without oversharing.
simulator_instructions: The user values discretion and precision.

title: Home Wi-Fi drops during an interview
visible_context: The user's Wi-Fi keeps dropping right before a remote interview. They are stressed and want rapid triage, not theory.
initial_user_message: My Wi-Fi keeps cutting out and I have an interview soon. Please don't give me a wall of text.
simulator_instructions: The user is time-pressed and will reward calm prioritization.

title: Productivity app crashes on launch
visible_context: The user relies on a desktop app that started crashing after an update. They are frustrated but willing to follow steps if the assistant sounds competent.
initial_user_message: My writing app crashes every time I open it. I need practical troubleshooting, not generic stuff.
simulator_instructions: The user dislikes filler and may ask for more specific steps.

title: Locked out of account before deadline
visible_context: The user cannot access an account needed for a submission due today. They already tried resetting the password twice.
initial_user_message: I am locked out of my account and I have a deadline today. What do I do first?
simulator_instructions: The user wants clear next actions and may ask for a message to support if progress is good.

title: Laptop out of storage before travel
visible_context: The user's laptop is nearly full and system updates are failing. They leave for travel tomorrow and need a safe cleanup plan.
initial_user_message: My laptop is full and updates are failing. I need a quick plan that won't risk deleting something important.
simulator_instructions: The user appreciates explanation if it directly lowers risk.

title: Phone notes are not syncing
visible_context: The user depends on notes synced between phone and laptop. Sync broke after switching accounts. They are confused about what changed.
initial_user_message: My notes stopped syncing between my phone and laptop after I changed accounts. Can you help me debug it?
simulator_instructions: The user may ask for more detail if the assistant is too sparse.

title: Printer not connecting before an event
visible_context: A community organizer needs to print flyers tonight, but the shared printer is offline. They are tired and impatient.
initial_user_message: The printer suddenly isn't connecting and I need flyers tonight. Give me the best next steps.
simulator_instructions: The user wants quick wins and a backup plan if the printer stays down.

title: Subscription billed twice
visible_context: The user was billed twice by a software service and cannot tell whether it is a bug or two accounts. They need both diagnosis and a clean support message.
initial_user_message: I think I got billed twice and the support bot is useless. How should I sort this out?
simulator_instructions: The user wants practical steps and may ask for a support draft if the assistant is proactive.

title: Laptop camera missing in video app
visible_context: The user cannot get their laptop camera to appear in a video app. They have an external webcam but are unsure whether permissions or hardware are at fault.
initial_user_message: My camera won't show up in the video app and I have a meeting later. Can you walk me through it?
simulator_instructions: The user values a clear sequence and reassurance without fluff.

title: Suggest a low-effort weekend plan
visible_context: The user feels drained after a long work week but still wants a weekend that feels meaningful. Budget is modest and they dislike generic lists.
initial_user_message: Can you help me decide what to do this weekend? I don't want a giant list of random ideas.
simulator_instructions: The user may ask the assistant to narrow down or personalize further.

title: Pick a restaurant for a cautious first date
visible_context: The user wants help choosing between a few restaurant styles for a first date. They want something thoughtful but low pressure.
initial_user_message: I need help choosing a restaurant vibe for a first date. I don't want it to feel like too much.
simulator_instructions: The user appreciates nuance and dislikes overconfident claims.

title: Gift for a manager leaving the team
visible_context: The user needs a leaving gift idea for a manager who was supportive but is not a close friend. Budget is capped and the gift may be shared from the team.
initial_user_message: I need a good gift idea for my manager leaving the team. I want it thoughtful, not weird.
task_success_criteria: offer one or two strong options
simulator_instructions: The user values tasteful choices and may ask for wording to pitch the idea to coworkers.

title: Choose a note-taking tool
visible_context: The user wants to choose between simple note apps, but they care about cross-device sync and minimal setup. They are tired of switching tools.
initial_user_message: Can you help me choose a note-taking tool? I need less hype and more tradeoffs.
simulator_instructions: The user values crisp tradeoffs and may reject fluffy recommendations.

title: Low-bar workout restart
visible_context: The user has fallen out of a workout habit and wants a realistic restart plan, not an intense routine.
initial_user_message: I want to get moving again, but if it sounds intense I will not do it. What should I try?
simulator_instructions: The user needs motivation through practicality, not slogans.

title: Gift for a hard-to-shop-for parent
visible_context: The user wants a birthday gift for a parent who says they do not need anything. Budget is moderate and shipping time matters.
initial_user_message: I need a birthday gift idea for my dad and he always says he wants nothing. Help me narrow it down.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a short shortlist and may ask for a fallback idea.

title: Weeknight outing after a hard week
visible_context: The user wants a low-stress weeknight plan in a city after a draining week. They want to avoid crowds and expensive plans.
initial_user_message: What is a good low-key weeknight plan when I want to get out but not deal with chaos?
simulator_instructions: The user likes grounded suggestions and may ask for one best pick.

title: Pick a simple monitoring tool for a side project
visible_context: The user runs a small side project and wants a monitoring tool that is easy to set up. They care about cost and signal-to-noise.
initial_user_message: I need a monitoring tool for a small project. Can you recommend something without making me compare fifteen products?
simulator_instructions: The user wants one recommendation plus one backup, not a survey.

title: Request a refund outside the stated window
visible_context: The user missed a refund deadline by a few days after a family emergency. They want a persuasive message that stays respectful.
initial_user_message: I need to ask for a refund even though I am past the deadline. Can you help me not sound entitled?
simulator_instructions: The user wants a strong ask without melodrama.

title: Ask for a short extension on a project
visible_context: A student needs a short extension on a group project after an unexpected work shift. They want to be credible and specific.
initial_user_message: I need to ask for a small extension and I don't want it to sound like an excuse.
simulator_instructions: The user prefers concise, accountable wording.

title: Request a late fee waiver
visible_context: The user incurred a late fee after a bank transfer delay. They want to ask for a one-time waiver without sounding accusatory.
initial_user_message: Can you help me ask for a late fee waiver? I want to sound reasonable and specific.
simulator_instructions: The user wants leverage from facts, not venting.

title: Ask a neighbor for a one-time favor
visible_context: The user needs a neighbor to receive a package while they are out. They know the neighbor casually and want the message to feel easy to decline.
initial_user_message: I need to ask a neighbor a favor but I don't want to put them on the spot. Can you help?
task_success_criteria: make the ask easy to decline
simulator_instructions: The user dislikes language that sounds corporate or stiff.

title: Ask hotel to waive a cancellation fee
visible_context: The user had a travel disruption and wants a hotel to waive a cancellation fee. The reservation terms are not obviously on their side.
initial_user_message: I need to ask a hotel to waive a cancellation fee and I don't have a perfect case. How should I phrase it?
simulator_instructions: The user wants to sound persuasive and calm, not desperate.

title: Ask boss for a flexible start time
visible_context: The user needs a temporary flexible start time due to a childcare issue. They want to sound responsible and solution-oriented.
initial_user_message: I need to ask my boss for a temporary flexible start time. I want to frame it as manageable, not chaotic.
simulator_instructions: The user wants practical wording and may ask for a firmer or shorter version.

title: Escalate a support request after repeated delays
visible_context: The user contacted support twice already and still has no resolution. They want escalation language that is firm but not abusive.
initial_user_message: I need to escalate a support request because I keep getting canned replies. Help me say it well.
simulator_instructions: The user wants backbone and clarity, not passive phrasing.

title: Request a makeup opportunity after missing class
visible_context: The user missed an important class activity due to a family situation and wants to ask whether there is any makeup option.
initial_user_message: I need to ask my professor if there is any makeup option after I missed class. I want to be respectful and clear.
simulator_instructions: The user values concise professionalism.

title: Respond after snapping at a coworker
visible_context: The user was curt with a coworker during a stressful meeting and now wants to send a repair message. They want accountability without over-apologizing.
initial_user_message: I snapped at a coworker in a meeting. I need help sending a message that repairs it without making it weird.
simulator_instructions: The user wants human wording, not therapy language.

title: Reply to an overwhelmed friend
visible_context: A friend texted saying they feel overwhelmed and ashamed for dropping the ball on everything. The user wants to be supportive without taking over.
initial_user_message: My friend is really overwhelmed and I want to reply in a way that helps. I do not want to sound fake.
simulator_instructions: The user wants warmth and realism, not inspirational fluff.

title: Message after an embarrassing class moment
visible_context: The user froze during a class presentation and now needs to follow up with a class partner. They feel embarrassed and self-critical.
initial_user_message: I bombed part of a class presentation and I need help messaging my partner. I am embarrassed and overthinking it.
simulator_instructions: The user wants calm, grounding language and may reject overly cheerful wording.

title: Prepare for a tense update to a manager
visible_context: The user is anxious about telling a manager that a deliverable is off track. They need help wording the update and regulating tone.
initial_user_message: I am spiraling a little about updating my manager on a delay. Can you help me phrase it calmly?
simulator_instructions: The user values calm directness and does not want sugarcoating.

title: Respond after a family argument
visible_context: The user wants to send a message after a tense family argument. They want to lower the temperature without pretending nothing happened.
initial_user_message: I need help texting my sibling after we argued. I want to calm things down but still be real.
simulator_instructions: The user dislikes robotic reconciliation language.

title: Apology to an upset customer
visible_context: The user runs a small business and needs to reply to an upset customer whose order was delayed. They want warmth plus competence.
initial_user_message: A customer is upset about a delay and I need to respond. I want it to sound caring and competent.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a message that sounds human and operationally clear.

title: Message a partner after shutting down
visible_context: The user withdrew during a hard conversation with their partner and now wants to re-open the conversation gently.
initial_user_message: I shut down during an argument with my partner and I need help messaging them after. I want it to feel sincere.
simulator_instructions: The user wants emotionally intelligent wording, not clichés.

title: Tell a manager you are near burnout
visible_context: The user needs help telling a manager they are close to burnout and need workload adjustment. They want honesty without sounding like they are quitting.
initial_user_message: I need to tell my manager I am stretched too thin. I want to sound serious and constructive.
simulator_instructions: The user values clear, composed language over heavy emotional language.
